title: COMMUNITY TRANSITIONAL SCHOOL - 211info
text: COMMUNITY TRANSITIONAL SCHOOL COMMUNITY TRANSITIONAL SCHOOL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A private school dedicated to serving children whose families are experiencing homelessness, housing transitions, or ongoing poverty-related crises. The school provides a stable learning environment with an individualized academic curriculum, breakfast, lunch, and snacks, medical and dental services and referrals, as well as clothing, shoes, basic needs items, and transportation to support consistent school attendance for children in transition. 6601 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Grades Pre-K–8 for children whose families are in transition, experiencing homelessness, or for unaccompanied youth. Homeless children and youth are individuals who lack a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence. This includes children and youth who share housing with others due to loss of housing or economic hardship, live in motels, parks, or campgrounds, have a primary nighttime residence that is not designed for sleeping, live in cars, abandoned buildings, substandard housing, or similar situations, or are migratory children living in circumstances like those described above. Unaccompanied youth are young people not in the physical custody of a parent or guardian who live on their own in any of the homeless situations described in the McKinney-Vento Homeless Education Act. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 249-8582 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 249-8582 FAX (503) 249-8633 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or to enroll over the phone FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus line 72 Services: Homeless School Transition Programs If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
